_comment: OpenClaw on Pterodactyl — headless Gateway with Discord + Telegram and a DeepSeek-style (OpenAI-compatible) model backend. Build/push the image from this repo's Dockerfile and set the docker image below to your registry.
name: OpenClaw
author: you@example.com
description: Self-hosted OpenClaw personal AI agent. Talks over Discord and Telegram, runs SKILL.md skills, and connects to any OpenAI-compatible LLM (DeepSeek by default). Config is generated headlessly from the variables below.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# OpenClaw install: the image bakes in OpenClaw itself, so installation only
# needs to pre-create the persistent directories on the server volume.
set -e
mkdir -p /mnt/server/.openclaw/workspace/skills
echo 'OpenClaw volume initialized. Configuration is generated on first boot from the egg variables.'

variables.name: LLM API Key
variables.description: API key for your model provider (DeepSeek, OpenRouter, OpenAI-compatible, etc.). Stored only in the environment; never written into the config file.

variables.name: LLM Provider ID
variables.description: Internal id for the provider block (the part before the slash in the model ref). e.g. deepseek, openrouter.

variables.name: LLM Base URL
variables.description: OpenAI-compatible base URL for the provider. DeepSeek: https://api.deepseek.com/v1 | OpenRouter: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1

variables.name: Primary Model
variables.description: Model id within the provider. Combined with the provider id as <provider>/<model>. DeepSeek: deepseek-chat or deepseek-reasoner.

variables.name: Fallback Models
variables.description: Optional comma-separated fallback model ids (provider prefix optional). e.g. deepseek-reasoner

variables.name: Model Context Window
variables.description: Context window (tokens) advertised for the model.

variables.name: Requires reasoning_content on replay
variables.description: Set 1 for backends (some DeepSeek-compatible proxies) that reject requests after reasoning content is stripped from prior assistant messages.

variables.name: Telegram Bot Token
variables.description: Token from @BotFather. Leave blank to disable the Telegram channel.

variables.name: Telegram Owner User ID
variables.description: Your numeric Telegram user ID (allowlisted to DM the bot, and set as owner). DM the bot and read from.id, or use the Bot API getUpdates.

variables.name: Discord Bot Token
variables.description: Bot token from the Discord Developer Portal. Requires the Message Content Intent (and Server Members Intent for role allowlists). Leave blank to disable Discord.

variables.name: Discord Owner User ID
variables.description: Your numeric Discord user ID (allowlisted to DM the bot, and set as owner).

variables.name: Skill Repositories
variables.description: Space- or comma-separated git URLs of skill repos, optionally pinned with #<ref>. Cloned into the workspace skills directory on boot. SECURITY: only use trusted, pinned sources.

variables.name: Gateway Bind Mode
variables.description: Network exposure for the Control UI / WebSocket gateway. 'loopback' (recommended) keeps it inside the container; chat channels still work since they connect outbound. Use 'lan' only with auth=token to reach the dashboard via the allocated port.

variables.name: Gateway Auth Mode
variables.description: Auth for the gateway/Control UI. Use 'none' only with bind=loopback. If bind=lan, set 'token' and provide OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN. The container refuses to start on an unsafe combination (non-loopback bind + none).

variables.name: Gateway Token
variables.description: Shared token used when Gateway Auth Mode is 'token' (read as OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN). Required to expose the dashboard on a LAN bind.

variables.name: Managed Config
variables.description: When 1 (default), openclaw.json is regenerated from these variables every boot. Set 0 to manage the config by hand via SFTP.
